definitions: The act or process of making something neutral.
A chemical reaction in which an acid and a base react to form a salt and water.
The process of making something ineffective or harmless.
The act of declaring a country or territory neutral in a conflict.
The process by which a speech sound loses its distinctive features in certain contexts.
examples: The neutralization of the acid was complete.
The treaty called for the neutralization of the border region.
The neutralization of the virus was a priority.
Phonologists study the neutralization of vowel sounds.
